Analysis

wk

2026-04-15

Data Steps

Data Description

#38005 total calves in the data set

full %>% 
  group_by(SEX) %>% 
  count()
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
## # Groups:   SEX [2]
##   SEX       n
##   <chr> <int>
## 1 F     31545
## 2 M      6460
#31545 heifers and 6460 male calves


full %>% 
  group_by(CBRD) %>% 
  count()
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
## # Groups:   CBRD [5]
##   CBRD      n
##   <chr> <int>
## 1 -        56
## 2 D      1691
## 3 H     29265
## 4 J       690
## 5 X      6303
full %>% 
  group_by(month) %>% 
  count()
## # A tibble: 12 × 2
## # Groups:   month [12]
##    month     n
##    <chr> <int>
##  1 Apr    2706
##  2 Aug    3490
##  3 Dec    2763
##  4 Feb    2713
##  5 Jan    3080
##  6 Jul    3734
##  7 Jun    2987
##  8 Mar    2794
##  9 May    2779
## 10 Nov    3310
## 11 Oct    3862
## 12 Sep    3787
full %>% 
  group_by(as.factor(comp)) %>% 
  count()
## # A tibble: 8 × 2
## # Groups:   as.factor(comp) [8]
##   `as.factor(comp)`     n
##   <fct>             <int>
## 1 1                  2557
## 2 2                  6362
## 3 3                  6238
## 4 4                  1750
## 5 5                  3529
## 6 6                  6310
## 7 7                  5997
## 8 8                  5262
full %>% 
  group_by(LOADfull) %>% 
  count()
## # A tibble: 229 × 2
## # Groups:   LOADfull [229]
##    LOADfull       n
##    <chr>      <int>
##  1 2024-11-12   174
##  2 2024-11-14   171
##  3 2024-11-16   189
##  4 2024-11-18   202
##  5 2024-11-20   187
##  6 2024-11-22   181
##  7 2024-11-24   180
##  8 2024-11-26   180
##  9 2024-11-28   177
## 10 2024-11-30   182
## # ℹ 219 more rows
full %>% 
  group_by(SRCE) %>% 
  count()
## # A tibble: 25 × 2
## # Groups:   SRCE [25]
##     SRCE     n
##    <int> <int>
##  1    11   600
##  2    14  2263
##  3    22   483
##  4    24  1158
##  5    29   991
##  6    31  2331
##  7    32   162
##  8    33   424
##  9    34   923
## 10    38   326
## # ℹ 15 more rows
full |> 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(TPROT))+
  theme_classic()+
  geom_histogram(binwidth=0.2, 
                 fill="purple3", 
                 color="black", 
                 alpha=0.7)+
  labs(title = "Histogram of stp")

full |> 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(diaM1_clean))+
  theme_classic()+
  geom_histogram(binwidth=0.2, 
                 fill="skyblue", 
                 color="black", 
                 alpha=0.7)+
  labs(title = "Histogram of diaM1")

full |> 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(diaM2_clean))+
  theme_classic()+
  geom_histogram(binwidth=0.2, 
                 fill="skyblue", 
                 color="black", 
                 alpha=0.7)+
  labs(title = "Histogram of diaM2")

full |> 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(wt1_clean))+
  theme_classic()+
  geom_histogram(binwidth=5, 
                 fill="red4", 
                 color="black", 
                 alpha=0.7)+
  labs(title = "Histogram of wt1")

full |> 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(wt2_clean))+
  theme_classic()+
  geom_histogram(binwidth=5, 
                 fill="red4", 
                 color="black", 
                 alpha=0.7)+
  labs(title = "Histogram of wt2")

full |> 
  ggplot(mapping=aes(adg))+
  theme_classic()+
  geom_histogram(binwidth=0.1, 
                 fill="wheat", 
                 color="black", 
                 alpha=0.7)+
  labs(title = "Histogram of adg (kg/d)")

Question: Are baseline characteristics of calves different by 1) different compartments; and 2) different SD quartiles (using quart_custom) 3) source farm?

full %>% 
  group_by(as.factor(comp)) %>% 
  summarize(
    stp = mean(TPROT, na.rm=TRUE), 
    diaM1 = mean(diaM1_clean, na.rm=TRUE), 
    wt1 = mean(wt1_clean, na.rm=TRUE), 
    diaload = mean(diaload, na.rm=TRUE))
## # A tibble: 8 × 5
##   `as.factor(comp)`   stp diaM1   wt1 diaload
##   <fct>             <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 1                  6.48 0.971  86.5    3.44
## 2 2                  6.35 0.890  87.2    3.22
## 3 3                  6.47 0.973  87.4    3.33
## 4 4                  6.47 0.930  86.6    3.48
## 5 5                  6.38 0.851  84.9    3.31
## 6 6                  6.23 0.769  82.3    3.19
## 7 7                  6.38 0.838  84.2    3.23
## 8 8                  6.49 0.913  86.3    3.34
table(full$comp, full$SEX)
##    
##        F    M
##   1 1973  584
##   2 4329 2033
##   3 5102 1136
##   4 1370  380
##   5 2904  625
##   6 6134  176
##   7 5540  457
##   8 4193 1069
table(full$comp, full$CBRD)
##    
##        -    D    H    J    X
##   1   14  145 1745   84  569
##   2   20  302 4023   29 1988
##   3   14  397 4630   37 1160
##   4    0   69 1279   39  363
##   5    0  102 2748   71  608
##   6    2  199 5772  194  143
##   7    5  315 5061  178  438
##   8    1  162 4007   58 1034
table(full$comp, full$month)
##    
##     Apr Aug Dec Feb Jan Jul Jun Mar May Nov Oct Sep
##   1 204 102 274 255 303 139 288 238 203 241 167 143
##   2 390 682 431 403 451 655 426 429 445 565 739 746
##   3 428 655 425 382 452 663 423 417 430 546 721 696
##   4 149  57 169 232 239 196 278 180 110 123  17   0
##   5 278 279 275 263 290 257 272 276 274 319 386 360
##   6 439 663 415 390 461 642 433 428 449 556 722 712
##   7 402 629 399 397 449 611 430 394 431 499 678 678
##   8 416 423 375 391 435 571 437 432 437 461 432 452
lm_1 <- lm(TPROT ~ as.factor(comp), data=full)
summary(lm_1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = TPROT ~ as.factor(comp), data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -5.1333 -0.5891 -0.0455  0.5545  5.9243 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       6.475714   0.017310 374.102  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)2 -0.130225   0.020495  -6.354 2.12e-10 ***
## as.factor(comp)3 -0.010629   0.020554  -0.517    0.605    
## as.factor(comp)4 -0.005085   0.027156  -0.187    0.851    
## as.factor(comp)5 -0.090732   0.022732  -3.991 6.58e-05 ***
## as.factor(comp)6 -0.242417   0.020520 -11.814  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)7 -0.097458   0.020674  -4.714 2.44e-06 ***
## as.factor(comp)8  0.013378   0.021101   0.634    0.526    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.8753 on 37997 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.009711,   Adjusted R-squared:  0.009528 
## F-statistic: 53.23 on 7 and 37997 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
lm_2 <- lm(diaM1_clean ~ as.factor(comp), data=full)
summary(lm_2)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = diaM1_clean ~ as.factor(comp), data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -0.9728  0.0272  0.0867  0.1624  3.2314 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       0.971020   0.009879  98.289  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)2 -0.081207   0.011701  -6.940 3.98e-12 ***
## as.factor(comp)3  0.001799   0.011731   0.153  0.87813    
## as.factor(comp)4 -0.040774   0.015433  -2.642  0.00824 ** 
## as.factor(comp)5 -0.120334   0.012959  -9.286  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)6 -0.202412   0.011721 -17.269  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)7 -0.133454   0.011811 -11.299  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)8 -0.057757   0.012059  -4.790 1.68e-06 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.4958 on 37342 degrees of freedom
##   (655 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.01866,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.01848 
## F-statistic: 101.4 on 7 and 37342 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
lm_3 <- lm(wt1_clean ~ as.factor(comp), data=full)
summary(lm_3)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = wt1_clean ~ as.factor(comp), data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -54.246  -8.152  -0.246   7.746  61.501 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       86.4994     0.2509 344.748  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)2   0.6524     0.2972   2.195  0.02816 *  
## as.factor(comp)3   0.8802     0.2979   2.954  0.00314 ** 
## as.factor(comp)4   0.1055     0.3919   0.269  0.78778    
## as.factor(comp)5  -1.6313     0.3291  -4.957 7.21e-07 ***
## as.factor(comp)6  -4.2450     0.2977 -14.260  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)7  -2.2538     0.3000  -7.513 5.89e-14 ***
## as.factor(comp)8  -0.2011     0.3063  -0.657  0.51147    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 12.59 on 37342 degrees of freedom
##   (655 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.02025,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.02007 
## F-statistic: 110.3 on 7 and 37342 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
lm_4 <- lm(diaload ~ as.factor(comp), data=full)
summary(lm_4)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = diaload ~ as.factor(comp), data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##    Min     1Q Median     3Q    Max 
## -3.188 -0.333 -0.224  0.667  2.812 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       3.44349    0.01420 242.414  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)2 -0.21950    0.01682 -13.051  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)3 -0.11053    0.01687  -6.553 5.71e-11 ***
## as.factor(comp)4  0.04108    0.02228   1.844   0.0653 .  
## as.factor(comp)5 -0.13037    0.01865  -6.989 2.82e-12 ***
## as.factor(comp)6 -0.25601    0.01684 -15.203  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)7 -0.20937    0.01697 -12.341  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(comp)8 -0.10046    0.01732  -5.802 6.61e-09 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.7183 on 37997 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.01346,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.01327 
## F-statistic: 74.04 on 7 and 37997 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
full %>% 
  group_by(quart_custom) %>% 
  summarize(
    stp = mean(TPROT, na.rm=TRUE), 
    diaM1 = mean(diaM1_clean, na.rm=TRUE), 
    wt1 = mean(wt1_clean, na.rm=TRUE), 
    diaload = mean(diaload, na.rm=TRUE))
## # A tibble: 4 × 5
##   quart_custom   stp diaM1   wt1 diaload
##   <chr>        <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1 High          6.42 0.926  86.7    3.35
## 2 Low           6.44 0.905  85.5    3.28
## 3 Med           6.37 0.846  85.4    3.31
## 4 Medlow        6.35 0.861  84.8    3.23
lm_1 <- lm(TPROT ~ quart_custom, data=full)
summary(lm_1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = TPROT ~ quart_custom, data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -5.2679 -0.6184 -0.0463  0.5639  5.9639 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                     Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)         6.418404   0.009921 646.968  < 2e-16 ***
## quart_customLow     0.017714   0.013300   1.332  0.18293    
## quart_customMed    -0.050478   0.013473  -3.746  0.00018 ***
## quart_customMedlow -0.072077   0.012979  -5.554 2.82e-08 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.8788 on 38001 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.001797,   Adjusted R-squared:  0.001718 
## F-statistic:  22.8 on 3 and 38001 DF,  p-value: 9.695e-15
lm_2 <- lm(diaM1_clean ~ quart_custom, data=full)
summary(lm_2)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = diaM1_clean ~ quart_custom, data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.92592  0.07408  0.09463  0.13948  3.15411 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                     Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)         0.925916   0.005665 163.449  < 2e-16 ***
## quart_customLow    -0.020543   0.007611  -2.699  0.00695 ** 
## quart_customMed    -0.080031   0.007702 -10.391  < 2e-16 ***
## quart_customMedlow -0.065399   0.007436  -8.795  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.4995 on 37346 degrees of freedom
##   (655 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.003975,   Adjusted R-squared:  0.003895 
## F-statistic: 49.68 on 3 and 37346 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
lm_3 <- lm(wt1_clean ~ quart_custom, data=full)
summary(lm_3)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = wt1_clean ~ quart_custom, data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -56.726  -7.795  -0.356   7.644  63.205 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)         86.7264     0.1441 601.972  < 2e-16 ***
## quart_customLow     -1.2092     0.1936  -6.247 4.22e-10 ***
## quart_customMed     -1.3701     0.1959  -6.994 2.71e-12 ***
## quart_customMedlow  -1.9314     0.1891 -10.213  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 12.7 on 37346 degrees of freedom
##   (655 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.002848,   Adjusted R-squared:  0.002768 
## F-statistic: 35.55 on 3 and 37346 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
lm_4 <- lm(diaload ~ quart_custom, data=full)
summary(lm_4)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = diaload ~ quart_custom, data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.3131 -0.3131 -0.2311  0.6869  2.7689 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)         3.34731    0.00815 410.726  < 2e-16 ***
## quart_customLow    -0.06296    0.01093  -5.763 8.35e-09 ***
## quart_customMed    -0.03421    0.01107  -3.091    0.002 ** 
## quart_customMedlow -0.11617    0.01066 -10.896  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.7219 on 38001 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.003483,   Adjusted R-squared:  0.003404 
## F-statistic: 44.27 on 3 and 38001 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
full %>% 
  group_by(SRCE) %>% 
  count()
## # A tibble: 25 × 2
## # Groups:   SRCE [25]
##     SRCE     n
##    <int> <int>
##  1    11   600
##  2    14  2263
##  3    22   483
##  4    24  1158
##  5    29   991
##  6    31  2331
##  7    32   162
##  8    33   424
##  9    34   923
## 10    38   326
## # ℹ 15 more rows
full %>% 
  group_by(SRCE) %>% 
  summarize(
    stp = mean(TPROT, na.rm=TRUE), 
    diaM1 = mean(diaM1_clean, na.rm=TRUE), 
    wt1 = mean(wt1_clean, na.rm=TRUE), 
    diaload = mean(diaload, na.rm=TRUE))

table(full$SRCE, full$SEX)

##   63 313 353 282 360 356 345 292 315 312 327 326 357

lm_1 <- lm(TPROT ~ as.factor(SRCE), data=full)
summary(lm_1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = TPROT ~ as.factor(SRCE), data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -4.8399 -0.5369 -0.0319  0.5071  5.6073 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)        6.73650    0.03328 202.390  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)14 -0.61357    0.03744 -16.389  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)22 -0.59447    0.04984 -11.927  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)24  0.05616    0.04101   1.369  0.17089    
## as.factor(SRCE)29 -0.88574    0.04217 -21.002  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)31 -0.49407    0.03732 -13.238  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)32 -1.27724    0.07219 -17.693  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)33 -0.07777    0.05173  -1.504  0.13270    
## as.factor(SRCE)34 -0.55546    0.04276 -12.991  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)38 -0.54386    0.05610  -9.695  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)40 -1.01386    0.08590 -11.803  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)55 -0.61176    0.04432 -13.802  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)60 -0.09963    0.03677  -2.710  0.00673 ** 
## as.factor(SRCE)61  0.26335    0.04011   6.566 5.25e-11 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)62 -0.70317    0.47189  -1.490  0.13621    
## as.factor(SRCE)63 -0.14356    0.03573  -4.018 5.88e-05 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)64  0.09539    0.03668   2.600  0.00932 ** 
## as.factor(SRCE)65 -0.47761    0.03571 -13.374  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)66 -0.40121    0.03710 -10.814  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)67  0.07313    0.03787   1.931  0.05351 .  
## as.factor(SRCE)71 -0.80007    0.04895 -16.345  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)72  0.08449    0.04391   1.924  0.05438 .  
## as.factor(SRCE)76 -0.79663    0.03535 -22.537  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)80 -0.48101    0.04091 -11.758  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)84 -0.41486    0.04212  -9.850  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.8153 on 37980 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1412, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1407 
## F-statistic: 260.2 on 24 and 37980 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
lm_2 <- lm(diaM1_clean ~ as.factor(SRCE), data=full)
summary(lm_2)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = diaM1_clean ~ as.factor(SRCE), data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.3308 -0.1658 -0.0005  0.1388  3.6610 
## 
## Coefficients:

## (Intercept)        1.000e+00  1.684e-02  59.370  < 2e-16 ***

## as.factor(SRCE)22  1.509e-01  2.518e-02   5.995 2.06e-09 ***

## as.factor(SRCE)71  2.544e-02  2.471e-02   1.029 0.303274    
## as.factor(SRCE)72 -2.603e-13  2.223e-02   0.000 1.000000    
## as.factor(SRCE)76 -1.388e-01  1.789e-02  -7.760 8.72e-15 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)80  4.639e-02  2.067e-02   2.244 0.024814 *  
## as.factor(SRCE)84 -1.017e-03  2.130e-02  -0.048 0.961908

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
##

##   (655 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.3333, Adjusted R-squared:  0.3329 
## F-statistic: 777.5 on 24 and 37325 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
lm_3 <- lm(wt1_clean ~ as.factor(SRCE), data=full)
summary(lm_3)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = wt1_clean ~ as.factor(SRCE), data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -53.222  -7.234  -0.153   7.136  64.685

## as.factor(SRCE)22  -1.64500    0.69274  -2.375 0.017572 *  
## as.factor(SRCE)24   1.18036    0.57069   2.068 0.038617 *  
## as.factor(SRCE)29  -5.01886    0.58512  -8.578  < 2e-16 ***

## as.factor(SRCE)64  -3.93793    0.51058  -7.713 1.26e-14 ***

## as.factor(SRCE)71   4.99556    0.67989   7.348 2.06e-13 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)72  -4.26431    0.61158  -6.973 3.16e-12 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)76  -5.72142    0.49210 -11.626  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)80   6.16284    0.56868  10.837  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)84  -1.45236    0.58601  -2.478 0.013202 *

## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
##

##   (655 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.2189, Adjusted R-squared:  0.2184 
## F-statistic: 435.9 on 24 and 37325 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
lm_4 <- lm(diaload ~ as.factor(SRCE), data=full)
summary(lm_4)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = diaload ~ as.factor(SRCE), data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.3657 -0.4570 -0.2362  0.5430  3.3321 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)        3.395000   0.026809 126.637  < 2e-16 ***

## as.factor(SRCE)22  0.141232   0.040144   3.518 0.000435 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)24  0.041960   0.033032   1.270 0.203987    
## as.factor(SRCE)29 -0.218411   0.033969  -6.430 1.29e-10 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)31 -0.479513   0.030062 -15.951  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)32  0.061790   0.058143   1.063 0.287914    
## as.factor(SRCE)33 -0.045943   0.041663  -1.103 0.270142    
## as.factor(SRCE)34 -0.158814   0.034437  -4.612 4.01e-06 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)38 -0.020767   0.045183  -0.460 0.645794    
## as.factor(SRCE)40 -0.130849   0.069188  -1.891 0.058602 .  
## as.factor(SRCE)55  0.004485   0.035699   0.126 0.900033    
## as.factor(SRCE)60  0.131779   0.029613   4.450 8.61e-06 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)61  0.191285   0.032306   5.921 3.23e-09 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)62 -0.728333   0.380081  -1.916 0.055340 .  
## as.factor(SRCE)63  0.006727   0.028779   0.234 0.815188    
## as.factor(SRCE)64 -0.727140   0.029548 -24.609  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)65 -0.586184   0.028763 -20.379  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)66  0.080748   0.029883   2.702 0.006894 ** 
## as.factor(SRCE)67  0.400187   0.030504  13.119  < 2e-16 ***
## as.factor(SRCE)71 -0.017093   0.039426  -0.434 0.664622    
## as.factor(SRCE)72  0.084012   0.035371   2.375 0.017545 *  
## as.factor(SRCE)76 -0.029292   0.028470  -1.029 0.303556    
## as.factor(SRCE)80  0.062021   0.032950   1.882 0.059807 .  
## as.factor(SRCE)84 -0.021253   0.033924  -0.626 0.531003    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.6567 on 37980 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1758, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1753 
## F-statistic: 337.6 on 24 and 37980 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

Pretty much all baseline characteristics are statistically significantly different between groups. However, a difference in days in age at movement or measure of 0.02 is not that important to me. Thus, I am comfortable that pretty much all baseline characteristics are similar.

Not sure entirely what to do with the source farms that were starting up or did not send calves on some months. Might mess up eventual model building so might have to exclude those calves? But if controlling for source farm as a random effect it might be OK? If I got rid of the farms that did not have at least 1 calf per month (29, 34, 40, 62) that would represent 2023 calves or 5% of total data.

Health Data

By each category, what is the average days post arrival for each disease? Also need to do this by compartment and by SD quartile.

full %>% 
  group_by(D30) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(DIAR30), 
    sd = sd(DIAR30))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##     D30 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 29866 30     0   
## 2     1  8139  8.53  4.28
full %>% 
  group_by(P30) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(PNEU30), 
    sd = sd(PNEU30))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##     P30 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 32905  30    0   
## 2     1  5100  20.1  6.33
full %>% 
  group_by(P60) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(PNEU60), 
    sd = sd(PNEU60))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##     P60 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 24208  60     0  
## 2     1 13797  34.9  13.6
full %>% 
  group_by(NAV30) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(NAVAL30), 
    sd = sd(NAVAL30))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##   NAV30 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 36102 30     0   
## 2     1  1903  8.64  6.88
full %>% 
  group_by(B30) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(BLOAT30), 
    sd = sd(BLOAT30))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##     B30 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 37094  30    0   
## 2     1   911  12.8  6.72
full %>% 
  group_by(IM30) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(ILLMISC30), 
    sd = sd(ILLMISC30))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##    IM30 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 37706 30     0   
## 2     1   299  9.98  8.49
full %>% 
  group_by(I10) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(INJURY10), 
    sd = sd(INJURY10))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##     I10 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 37933 10     0   
## 2     1    72  5.38  2.33
full %>% 
  group_by(L10) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(LAME10), 
    sd = sd(LAME10))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##     L10 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 37708 10    0    
## 2     1   297  1.03 0.182
full %>% 
  group_by(S30) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(SEPSIS30), 
    sd = sd(SEPSIS30))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##     S30 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 28413  30    0   
## 2     1  9592  12.8  6.29
full %>% 
  group_by(DED30) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(DIED30), 
    sd = sd(DIED30))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##   DED30 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 37295  30    0   
## 2     1   710  12.8  8.10
full %>% 
  group_by(DED60) %>% 
  summarize(
    count = n(), 
    mean = mean(DIED60), 
    sd = sd(DIED60))
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##   DED60 count  mean    sd
##   <int> <int> <dbl> <dbl>
## 1     0 36905  60     0  
## 2     1  1100  24.1  17.3

Continuous Outcome

ADG (should I take outcomes out to 90d?), association with month, comp, quart_custom? Or other baseline characteristics?

full %>% 
  group_by(month) %>% 
  summarize(
    adg = mean (adg, na.rm=TRUE))
## # A tibble: 12 × 2
##    month   adg
##    <chr> <dbl>
##  1 Apr   0.767
##  2 Aug   0.744
##  3 Dec   0.828
##  4 Feb   0.817
##  5 Jan   0.796
##  6 Jul   0.677
##  7 Jun   0.717
##  8 Mar   0.817
##  9 May   0.720
## 10 Nov   0.828
## 11 Oct   0.777
## 12 Sep   0.761
lm_1 <- lm(adg ~ month, data=full)
summary(lm_1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = adg ~ month, data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.68816 -0.07542 -0.00065  0.07316  0.67005 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.767209   0.002292 334.684  < 2e-16 ***
## monthAug    -0.023683   0.003117  -7.598 3.09e-14 ***
## monthDec     0.060505   0.003236  18.699  < 2e-16 ***
## monthFeb     0.049479   0.003246  15.243  < 2e-16 ***
## monthJan     0.028663   0.003148   9.106  < 2e-16 ***
## monthJul    -0.090385   0.003045 -29.683  < 2e-16 ***
## monthJun    -0.050242   0.003174 -15.829  < 2e-16 ***
## monthMar     0.049857   0.003216  15.501  < 2e-16 ***
## monthMay    -0.047437   0.003225 -14.708  < 2e-16 ***
## monthNov     0.061096   0.003775  16.186  < 2e-16 ***
## monthOct     0.009741   0.003380   2.882  0.00396 ** 
## monthSep    -0.006606   0.003019  -2.188  0.02869 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.1167 on 32525 degrees of freedom
##   (5468 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1421, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1418 
## F-statistic: 489.6 on 11 and 32525 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
#grew better in the winter, worse in the summer

full %>% 
  group_by(comp) %>% 
  summarize(
    adg = mean (adg, na.rm=TRUE))
## # A tibble: 8 × 2
##   comp    adg
##   <fct> <dbl>
## 1 1     0.778
## 2 2     0.787
## 3 3     0.767
## 4 4     0.771
## 5 5     0.765
## 6 6     0.754
## 7 7     0.750
## 8 8     0.762
lm_2 <- lm(adg ~ comp, data=full)
summary(lm_2)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = adg ~ comp, data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.68319 -0.08034 -0.00031  0.07987  0.73653 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.777528   0.002638 294.767  < 2e-16 ***
## comp2        0.009045   0.003145   2.876 0.004025 ** 
## comp3       -0.010614   0.003153  -3.366 0.000763 ***
## comp4       -0.006882   0.004050  -1.699 0.089256 .  
## comp5       -0.012478   0.003488  -3.577 0.000348 ***
## comp6       -0.023205   0.003150  -7.367 1.79e-13 ***
## comp7       -0.027311   0.003171  -8.614  < 2e-16 ***
## comp8       -0.015290   0.003224  -4.742 2.12e-06 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.1254 on 32529 degrees of freedom
##   (5468 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.009033,   Adjusted R-squared:  0.00882 
## F-statistic: 42.36 on 7 and 32529 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
#yes significant but nothing I'm particularly worried about

full %>% 
  group_by(quart_custom) %>% 
  summarize(
    adg = mean (adg, na.rm=TRUE))
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   quart_custom   adg
##   <chr>        <dbl>
## 1 High         0.766
## 2 Low          0.765
## 3 Med          0.767
## 4 Medlow       0.765
lm_3 <- lm(adg ~ quart_custom, data=full)
summary(lm_3)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = adg ~ quart_custom, data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.68569 -0.08039 -0.00050  0.07945  0.72058 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                      Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)         0.7661651  0.0015148 505.784   <2e-16 ***
## quart_customLow    -0.0014209  0.0020527  -0.692    0.489    
## quart_customMed     0.0007802  0.0020586   0.379    0.705    
## quart_customMedlow -0.0014338  0.0020052  -0.715    0.475    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.126 on 32533 degrees of freedom
##   (5468 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  5.807e-05,  Adjusted R-squared:  -3.413e-05 
## F-statistic: 0.6298 on 3 and 32533 DF,  p-value: 0.5957
#yes significant but nothing I'm particularly worried about

full %>% 
  group_by(SEX) %>% 
  summarize(
    adg = mean (adg, na.rm=TRUE))
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   SEX     adg
##   <chr> <dbl>
## 1 F     0.745
## 2 M     0.863
lm_4 <- lm(adg ~ SEX, data=full)
summary(lm_4)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = adg ~ SEX, data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.66592 -0.07372  0.00400  0.07919  0.74178 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.7449674  0.0007183 1037.13   <2e-16 ***
## SEXM        0.1180781  0.0017186   68.71   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.1177 on 32535 degrees of freedom
##   (5468 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1267, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1267 
## F-statistic:  4721 on 1 and 32535 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
#yes, bull calves or male castrated calves grew better than female calves

full %>% 
  group_by(CBRD) %>% 
  summarize(
    adg = mean (adg, na.rm=TRUE))
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   CBRD    adg
##   <chr> <dbl>
## 1 -     0.872
## 2 D     0.764
## 3 H     0.747
## 4 J     0.622
## 5 X     0.863
lm_5 <- lm(adg ~ CBRD, data=full)
summary(lm_5)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = adg ~ CBRD, data = full)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.66807 -0.07296  0.00365  0.07774  0.73962 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.871913   0.016174  53.907  < 2e-16 ***
## CBRDD       -0.108098   0.016462  -6.567 5.23e-11 ***
## CBRDH       -0.124793   0.016191  -7.707 1.32e-14 ***
## CBRDJ       -0.249603   0.016886 -14.781  < 2e-16 ***
## CBRDX       -0.008846   0.016250  -0.544    0.586    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.1166 on 32532 degrees of freedom
##   (5468 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1426, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1425 
## F-statistic:  1353 on 4 and 32532 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

KM Curves for Morbidity and Mortality

  1. Compartment

  2. SD

  3. Month

  4. Load

  5. Sex

  6. Breed

  7. Source Farm

30d SCOURS

surv_obj <- Surv(
  time  = full$DIAR30,
  event = full$D30)

#by compartment
fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ comp,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.6),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Scours Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 28),
  ylim = c(0.5, 1.0),
  legend = "top",
  break.time.by = 7)

table(full$D30)
## 
##     0     1 
## 29866  8139
#by stocking density quartile

fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ quart_custom,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.6),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Scours Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 28),
  ylim = c(0.5, 1.0),
  legend = "top",
  break.time.by = 7)

#month

fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ month,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.6),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Scours Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 28),
  ylim = c(0.5, 1.0),
  legend = "top",
  break.time.by = 7)

#by distinct load
fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ LOADfull,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.6),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Scours Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 28),
  legend = "none",
  break.time.by = 7)

#HUGE Variation!!!!



#sex
fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ SEX,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.6),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Scours Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 28),
  ylim = c(0.5, 1.0),
  legend = "top",
  break.time.by = 7)

#breed
fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ CBRD,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.6),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Scours Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 28),
  ylim = c(0.5, 1.0),
  legend = "top",
  break.time.by = 7)

#Source Farm
fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ SRCE,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.6),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Scours Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 28),
  ylim = c(0.5, 1.0),
  legend = "none",
  break.time.by = 7)

surv_obj <- Surv(
  time  = full$INJURY10,
  event = full$I10)

#by compartment
fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ comp,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.8),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Injury Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 10),
  ylim = c(0.8, 1.0),
  legend = "top",
  break.time.by = 2)

table(full$I10)
## 
##     0     1 
## 37933    72
#by stocking density quartile

fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ quart_custom,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.8),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Injury Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 10),
  ylim = c(0.8, 1.0),
  legend = "top",
  break.time.by = 2)

#month

fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ month,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.6),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Injury Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 10),
  ylim = c(0.5, 1.0),
  legend = "top",
  break.time.by = 2)

#by distinct load
fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ LOADfull,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.6),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Injury Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0.5, 10),
  legend = "none",
  break.time.by = 2)

#HUGE Variation!!!!

fit <- survfit(
  surv_obj ~ SEX,
  data = full)

ggsurvplot(
  fit,
  data = full,
  pval = TRUE,
  pval.coord = c(5, 0.6),
  conf.int = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Injury Treatment Survival Probability",
  xlim = c(0, 10),
  ylim = c(0.5, 1.0),
  legend = "top",
  break.time.by = 2)

  event = full$L10)

  surv_obj ~ comp,
  data = full)

  data = full,
